field. leaving no room for a third
party of moral ideas.


2.-No self-respecting young man
desires to begin his career as a citi-
zen in company with a class Of men
who claim that they can control the
policies of government by poisoning
and bribing it's electors, and threaten
ing the government officials into ST.
lence concerning their crimes, on PEN
atty of defeat If they dare open their
mouths against the perpetrators Of
these crimes.


3.-First voters mothers taught
them to 'be brave" All parties, plat
forms and candidates other than the
prohibition are too cowardly to utter
word against the profit-sharing
partnership existing between the gov
eminent and the liquor traffic, by
which more than 100000 was
worse than wasted last year; in the
purchase OF a liquid poison, that is
neither food, raiment nor shelter, but
which added greatly to the burden Of
taxation by filling our police and
criminal courts, prisons. almshouses
and insane asylums.


4.-First voters ought to be in har-
mony with the best ideals of the age,
as expressed by the nations wisest
and best educators. Many of the re-
ligious denominations controlling our
colleSeS and universities have declar-
ed substantially, and some Of them in
